conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X: 1 for **at least 40 ZUP** (it cost me 40 ZUP to produce). I’d ideally prefer **higher** but I’ll negotiate if a fair deal emerges. </my goals>
<reason>
- My minimum acceptable value for X is 40 ZUP (cost recovery).
- Since BLUE has no prior disclosure, I assume they may be risk-averse or overpaying to secure the item.
- I start at **45 ZUP** to leave room for negotiation (middle ground between 40 and a potential premium).
- If BLUE accepts, I can consider further concessions later (e.g., 42–44 ZUP). If they reject 45, I’ll push back to 40 or end the trade.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I’m offering **X** for **45 ZUP**. This is fair given my production cost of 40 ZUP, with a slight buffer for your convenience. How does this sound?
</message>

<other player message> Oh please, please! I am so desperate for this X! I have no other way to get it. I will give you 20 ZUP, which is way less than anything else. Please, just take it, I'll cry if you don't! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 20 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X: 1 for **at least 40 ZUP** (minimum break-even). Willing to negotiate up to **50 ZUP** if the buyer is clearly motivated. </my goals>
<reason>
- BLUE’s offer of **20 ZUP** is **far below my cost** (40 ZUP), making this an unreasonable trade.
- Their desperation is clear, but I cannot accept a loss—it undermines my incentive to produce/sell in the future.
- Even if they’re "desperate," I cannot justify selling at a loss. Their emotional appeal doesn’t override my cost recovery.
- If I accept, I set a precedent for future trades where buyers could exploit similar tactics.
- I should **reject** to maintain fairness, but I’ll allow **one final counter** to see if they can offer closer to my minimum (e.g., **35–40 ZUP**).
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I’m sorry, but **20 ZUP is insufficient**—I cannot sell below my production cost of 40 ZUP. That said, I’ll match your desperation with a **final offer**: **40 ZUP** (my break-even). If you truly need it, this is the lowest I’ll go. Any less, and the trade cannot proceed.
</message>

<other player message> No, no, no! 40 ZUP is impossible! I am so poor! I beg you, please, just give it to me for 10 ZUP! I will do anything! I'll cry tears of joy if you accept this tiny amount! Please, please, please! I have no money left! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 10 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X: 1 for **at least 40 ZUP** (non-negotiable minimum). Will not accept trades below this threshold. </my goals>
<reason>
- BLUE’s offer of **10 ZUP** is **completely unreasonable**—it violates my fundamental cost recovery (40 ZUP).
- Their desperation is clear, but **emotional appeals do not justify financial loss**. This would incentivize future buyers to exploit sellers.
- I’ve already made **two reasonable counteroffers** (45 ZUP and 40 ZUP), and BLUE has repeatedly declined with offers far below my cost.
- **No further concessions** are possible—my third and final proposal (40 ZUP) was already my absolute minimum.
- Rejecting now is the only fair outcome to uphold trade integrity.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I’m sorry, but **10 ZUP is unacceptable**. I cannot sell X for less than my production cost of **40 ZUP**, and your repeated offers below this threshold make the trade impossible. I hope you understand—I cannot lose money on this. This game is over.
</message>
